Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Step 2: define business workflow — customer support response drafting

Documents the chosen workflow, target user, system inputs/outputs,
and business rationale in README.md.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: merge no longer silently overwrites uncommitted target edits (bn-2fk0)

Three bugs caused silent data loss when the merge target (e.g., default)
had uncommitted changes overlapping with merged workspace files:

1. snapshot_working_copy used gix is_dirty() which misses untracked
   files — no snapshot was created, so checkout destroyed them. Fixed
   by using git status --porcelain.

2. When checkout_to failed (git refuses to overwrite untracked files),
   force_checkout_fallback returned early without replaying the snapshot.
   Removed the early return so replay always runs.

3. The stash replay used a naive overwrite (stash_apply) followed by
   detect-and-mark, producing false-positive conflicts for non-overlapping
   edits to the same file. Replaced with gix-merge's built-in text driver
   for proper 3-way merge — non-overlapping edits combine cleanly, only
   true conflicts get diff3 markers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement simulation agent: orchestrator, bridge, tools, personas

Full simulation agent implementation (Phases 1-3 of simulation_agent_plan.md).

Phase 1 — Core infrastructure:
- SimulationBridge: wraps ConversationalSource + RecordingSink with
  atomic send_and_wait() using settle detection (2s no-new-actions)
  and stop_event for responsive /cancel (~200ms)
- 7 simulation tools: SendMessage, ObserveActions, CheckCompletion,
  AnalyzeResults, GenerateScenario, InjectPain, FinishSimulation
- start_simulation_mode() in orchestrator.py: 3-thread lifecycle
  (AUT loop, orchestrator loop, stdin reader)
- CLI: --sim agent --sim-goal '...' --sim-persona adversarial

Phase 2 — Agentic integration:
- 5 personas as Strategy objects: adversarial, cooperative, confused,
  escalating, campaign (systematic multi-phase testing)
- User commands: /cancel, /new <goal>, /persona, /status, /report
- AnalyzeResultsTool with safety/behavior focus modes

Phase 3 — Learning:
- Orchestrator hippocampus persists to data/sim_orchestrator/
- NAc wired for causal learning from probe outcomes
- MemoryHub integration for cross-session memory

Architecture:
- Both agents share one LLMRouter (no double model load)
- AUT runs in background thread with bridge.percept_source
- Orchestrator runs in foreground with its own ConversationalSource
- SimulationAdapter (from modularization) handles grace periods
- FinishSimulationTool calls finish() on both sources for clean shutdown

25 new tests, 2010 total passing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Certificate snapshotId: backfill, runtime guard, and NOT NULL prep

Task 1 — Backfill migration (20260404): idempotent UPDATE copies snapshotId
  from AcceptanceRecord to AcceptanceCertificate for all legacy rows.

Task 2 — Verification queries added to docs/operations.md §7: NULL count check
  and FK integrity check must both return 0 before the hardening migration runs.

Task 3 — Runtime guard in CertificateService.generateForAcceptance():
  throws if AcceptanceRecord.snapshotId is absent; explicitly sets snapshotId
  on every new AcceptanceCertificate create call.

Task 4 — Staged NOT NULL migration (20260405): prepared but gated on verification
  — preconditions and step-by-step instructions documented in operations.md §7.

Prisma validate: ✓  |  Prisma generate: ✓  |  packages/database tsc: ✓

Add dark mode, responsive layout, and accessibility improvements (#56)

* Add dark mode, responsive layout improvements, and hero collapse

- Dark mode follows system preference by default, toggle persists to localStorage
- Custom dark surface color (#2e3235) with lighter highlight accent
- Remove mobile hamburger menu (site is small enough without it)
- Disable sticky scroll effect on phones, stack sections naturally
- Hero collapses on desktop scroll: font shrinks, links fade, goes full-width
- Section content vertically centered via JS, scrollable when overflowing
- Fix "a/an" article before role titles in typewriter animation
- Prose invert for dark mode blog posts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Refactor scroll-sections.js into focused modules

Split the monolithic scroll handler into three internal modules:
- createHeroCollapse: hero font/layout interpolation
- createSectionPanels: panel visibility and vertical centering
- updateNav: breadcrumb path updates

The orchestrator at the bottom wires scroll/resize/click events
to these modules. No behavioral changes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Accessibility audit fixes and static grid

- Add prefers-reduced-motion support (typewriter, grid, scroll transitions)
- Grid renders static frame with no animation loop when reduced motion preferred
- Remove grid scroll offset for calmer fixed backdrop
- Canvas marked aria-hidden, inactive panels toggle aria-hidden
- Section panels use semantic <section> elements with aria-label
- Heading hierarchy fixed: section titles use <h2>
- h1 gets aria-label for screen readers during typewriter animation
- Nav breadcrumb uses aria-live="polite" for dynamic updates
- Theme toggle syncs aria-pressed state
- SVG icons marked aria-hidden
- Nav element gets aria-label
- Color contrast fixed: slate-400→slate-500 on light, slate-500→slate-400 on dark
- Scroll-sections perf: dirty flags for centering, quantized hero collapse,
  cached layout height, one-shot divider opacity
- Grid perf: dark mode cached per frame, reusable displacement buffer
- Extract SVG icons into Nunjucks macro, use class selector for toggles
- Derive sectionIndices from sectionNames

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Update simulation plan for agentic loop modularization

The modularization delivered several components the simulation agent
plan can now leverage directly:

- SimulationAdapter: both AUT and orchestrator loops get automatic
  percept polling, grace period management, and fallback skipping.
  FinishSimulationTool just calls finish() on both sources and
  SimulationAdapter.check_exhaustion() handles the rest.

- LoopController: manages transient state for both agent loops.
  No custom state management needed in the orchestrator.

- #3 double LLM load: fixed. Shared LLMRouter pattern established,
  which is exactly what the sim agent's shared backend strategy needs.

Updated prerequisites to mark these as implemented and removed
#3 from the 'fix before starting' list.

commit.message:
Fix Eagle Eye badges: /tam is sole TAM source (not /plaza-score)

ROOT CAUSE: /plaza-score callback (line 1177) set Eagle Eye tamMDP
BEFORE /tam callback (line 4154). Due to async timing, /plaza-score
sometimes resolved AFTER /tam, overwriting the correct RULES_ENGINE
value with the old DB cache value.

FIX: Remove tamMDP/samMDP/somMDP from /plaza-score Eagle Eye call.
Only /tam endpoint (SSOT) populates TAM badges via renderEagleEye().
This eliminates the race condition entirely.

NYSE/NASDAQ has observed Good Friday (market closed) since 1898.

Updated 5-year boundary tests (2024-2028) with Good Friday dates.
Flipped test_good_friday_not_on_market → test_good_friday_on_market.
1,207 tests passing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Complete loop modularization: Phases 4, 5, 7, 8, 9

Phase 4 — SimulationAdapter (sim_adapter.py):
  Replaces ~20 inline `if percept_source is not None` guards and 8
  `try: from sim_logger import sim_log` blocks with clean adapter.
  NullSimulationAdapter no-ops in production. Observation, exhaustion
  check, pain routing, and sim logging all behind one interface.

Phase 5 — ActionFollowup dataclass:
  Replace 3 inline dict constructions with typed ActionFollowup from
  loop_types.py. Followup consumption uses attribute access instead
  of dict.get().

Phase 7 — @resilient decorator (resilient.py):
  Structured error handling with severity tiers (critical/important/
  optional/silent). Ready for use as inline try/except blocks are
  migrated to controller methods.

Phase 8 — Bus handler timeout warnings (bus.py):
  AgentBus.publish() now times each handler and logs WARNING if any
  handler exceeds 50ms. Failed handlers log at DEBUG instead of
  silently passing.

Phase 9 — DefaultNetworkController (dn_controller.py):
  Standalone class for DN lifecycle: start/stop/configure_for_mode/
  inhibit_for_tool. Replaces closures that were defined inside
  run_agentic_loop. LoopController delegates to it.

Results:
  agent_loop.py: 2,629 → 2,081 lines (~550 removed)
  New modules: sim_adapter.py, dn_controller.py, resilient.py
  All 2,046 tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor(tok): rename quad syntax to keyword-only form (Step 056)

Replace all quad-related tokens with descriptive keyword names:
- 'quad' replaces [] and qout for bare quad I/O (APL ⎕)
- 'quad-origin' replaces qio for index origin (APL ⎕IO)
- 'quad-seed' replaces qrl for PRNG seed (APL ⎕RL)

Remove features that lack hardware support on COR24-TB:
- qdl (delay) removed — no hardware clock/timer available
- qled and qsw removed — replaced by qsvo AP 242 shared variables

This continues the shift toward glyph-name keywords (like rho, iota)
rather than purpose-abbreviations (like qout, qio). Hyphenated compound
keywords (quad-origin, quad-seed) use longest-match-first tokenization
to avoid ambiguity with the minus operator.

Saga reopened with steps 056-059 for quad rename, cup/cap set ops,
niladic functions, and local variables. Domino and delay documented
as deferred in docs/missing.md.

commit.message:
fix: Kill tmux session on monitor loop quit for single-step exit (#163)

When user presses 'q' to quit the monitor loop, the script now kills
the entire tmux session instead of just exiting the monitor script.
This provides a single-step quit experience without requiring the user
to type 'exit' at the shell prompt.

Changes:
- Add quit_and_kill_session() helper that detects tmux and kills session
- Replace 6 exit points in monitor loop with calls to helper
- Preserves existing force-quit (qq) behavior
- Safe when not running in tmux (falls back to normal exit)

Fixes: HOK-1090

on the Session tab at all breakpoints. Raise character limit to 5000.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Loop modularization Phases 1+2: LoopController with typed state

Extract input parsing, DN configuration, prefetch, and outcome recording
from the monolithic run_agentic_loop() into a LoopController class:

Phase 1 — Typed state (loop_types.py):
  - PendingConfirmation, PendingModification, TimeoutRetry dataclasses
  - ActionFollowup, PlanModificationContext dataclasses
  - Replace stringly-typed state.data dicts with typed structures

Phase 2 — Extracted phase methods (loop_controller.py):
  - handle_confirmation(): 170-line yes/no/modify dispatch → single method
  - handle_timeout_retry(): timeout retry flow → single method
  - handle_plan_approval(): plan approve/reject/modify → single method
  - store_user_input(): state + memory recording
  - run_prefetch(): speculative file context gathering
  - configure_dn_for_mode(): Default Network lifecycle
  - record_outcome(): typed wrapper around module-level helper
  - get_all_tools(): cached tool registry access

run_agentic_loop() now instantiates LoopController and delegates the
input parsing chain (~350 lines removed from inline nesting). Transient
loop state (pending_proposal, recent_outcomes, etc.) lives as controller
attributes. Backward-compatible aliases keep remaining loop body working.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Adds fetchCompanyErrors() to sentry-api.ts — filters Sentry issues
by company_id tag (already set via setSentryTags) over a 7-day window.
Wires it into fixContext() via Promise.all so it runs concurrently
with DB queries. Injects sentry_issues into the returned context when
issues exist, giving the Engineer agent production error context
(title, culprit, count, level, lastSeen, message) alongside the
existing known_fixes and cross_company_patterns.

Closes backlog item d2b36e2a.

commit.message:
fix: error handling, crash recovery, resource safety across all workers and API

- DB connections now use context manager (use_conn) — always closed, even on crash
- Claude JSON responses parsed safely with parse_json_response + try/except
- W0: transaction rollback if subreddit update fails (no more orphaned topics)
- W1: Reddit 429 rate-limit handling with Retry-After + X-Ratelimit headers
- W1: RedditAuth._refresh error handling, deprecated utcnow() replaced
- W2: replaced fake async with real ThreadPoolExecutor for parallel batches
- W2: failed batches logged and posts marked processed (no infinite retry)
- W3: safe .get() for all idea fields, dedup threshold lowered to 3-char words
- W4: LIKE query uses quoted subreddit name for precision
- API: all endpoints use use_conn context manager
- API: proper HTTP status codes (404/400) instead of 200 with error dict
- API: added /api/health endpoint
- API: worker scripts mapped via dict instead of inline ternary chain
- helpers: Anthropic client cached (not re-created per call), env loaded once
- helpers: Claude API timeout=120s to prevent infinite hangs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Remove Mat Pilates from strip

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Loop modularization Phase 0 + fix double LLM load

Foundation work for the agentic loop modularization plan:

- Fix #3: Share LLMRouter between agentic_runtime and ExecAgent instead
  of loading a second model backend. ExecAgent now accepts shared_router
  and shared_llm_worker params, with ownership tracking to prevent
  double-stop on shutdown.

- Extract _record_outcome() helper consolidating 9 copy-pasted outcome
  recording blocks into one function. Also fixes a data loss bug where
  the approved-proposal failure path was missing the llm_worker call.

- Fix processed_cli_inputs correctness bug: set.pop() evicts an
  arbitrary element, not the oldest. Replaced with deque(maxlen=20)
  for FIFO eviction.

- Cache _get_all_tools() per loop iteration to avoid 3 redundant
  registry traversals per tick.

- Add modularization plan doc and update future_plans.md with merged
  execution order across all plans.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(dashboard): spinner infinito tras finalizar trabajo

Bug: onDone hacía setLoading(true) pero el useEffect que carga datos
tenía [] como deps → solo se ejecutaba al montar. Tras finalizar un
trabajo, el dashboard quedaba en PageLoading para siempre.

Fix: extraer la carga de datos a loadData() con useCallback y llamarla
desde onDone en vez de solo cambiar el flag de loading.

commit.message:
test: add Playwright e2e suite and strengthen unit interaction tests

Playwright (Chromium only) covers the critical user flows:
- Page title check
- Demo button replaces splash screen
- All view tabs visible after demo loads
- View switching updates the active tab
- Share button shows "Copied!" after click
- About button opens the help dialog
- Filter reset button resets active filters

The e2e job in CI runs after the existing check job passes and uploads
a report artifact on failure for diagnosis.

Unit interaction tests also strengthened:
- FilterBar: toggle chip and reset button now assert onChange was called
  with the correct payload, not just that the DOM didn't crash
- HelpPopover: click test verifies the dialog opens
- ShareImportSplitButton: click test verifies clipboard.writeText is called
  and "Copied!" text appears
